Stucco Repair in Travis County, TX

From hairline cracks spreading across your Austin home to soft, bubbling stucco near your windows, Star Stucco of Austin delivers lasting repairs that match your existing finish. Our crews work throughout Travis County, addressing damage caused by the intense Central Texas sun and seasonal storms that push moisture behind exterior walls.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

Why Travis County Stucco Takes a Beating

Travis County sits at the heart of Central Texas, where summer temperatures regularly exceed 100 degrees and sudden thunderstorms can dump inches of rain in under an hour. That cycle of extreme heat followed by heavy moisture creates the ideal conditions for stucco cracking, delamination, and water intrusion — problems we see on homes from Rollingwood to Pflugerville.

The expansive clay soils common throughout the Austin metro area add another layer of stress. As the ground swells during wet periods and contracts during drought, foundations shift and stucco cracks follow. Our repair process accounts for this soil movement, using flexible bonding methods that accommodate the natural settling unique to Travis County properties.

Whether your home overlooks Lake Travis or sits in one of the rapidly growing neighborhoods east of I-35, our team has the local experience to diagnose exactly what is causing your stucco damage and fix it with materials and techniques proven to hold up in this climate.

What's Included With Our Stucco Repair in Travis County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

Comprehensive Damage Assessment

We inspect your entire exterior, mapping every crack, soft spot, and area of discoloration so nothing gets overlooked before we begin.

Moisture Testing

Using a professional-grade moisture meter, we check behind the stucco surface to identify hidden water intrusion that could lead to mold or structural decay.

Damaged Material Removal

All compromised stucco is carefully removed down to sound substrate, ensuring the new material bonds properly and the repair lasts.

Substrate Repair

If the lath, wire, or sheathing beneath the stucco has been damaged by moisture or movement, we repair or replace it before applying new material.

Color & Texture Matching

We custom-mix our repair material to match the existing color and replicate the original texture so the patch blends in rather than standing out.

Finish Application & Sealing

The final coat is applied, textured to match, and sealed to resist the UV exposure and rain cycles that Travis County homes endure year-round.

Common Stucco Repair Needs in Travis County

These are the most frequent stucco repair issues we encounter on Travis County properties. Each one calls for a different approach, and getting the diagnosis right is the key to a repair that lasts.

Window & Door Frame Cracking

Stucco around openings is under constant stress from thermal expansion and foundation settling. We remove the failed material, seal the joint properly, and rebuild the stucco to prevent moisture from working its way inside the wall.

Water Damage Restoration

Heavy spring rains drive water behind stucco through failed sealant and hairline cracks. We trace the entry point, dry the wall cavity, treat for mold if needed, and rebuild the damaged section from the substrate out.

Patch Failure Repair

Previous repairs that were applied over damaged substrate or without proper bonding agents tend to pop off within a few seasons. We strip the failed patch, prepare the surface correctly, and apply a lasting fix.

Stucco Delamination

When stucco separates from the underlying lath, it creates hollow sections that sound different when tapped. Left alone, delaminated areas eventually crack and fall away. We reattach or replace the affected sections to restore a solid wall.

Efflorescence & Staining

White mineral deposits and dark staining indicate moisture is migrating through the stucco. We clean the surface, identify the moisture source, and seal the wall to stop the cycle.

Impact Damage

Hail, fallen branches, and landscaping equipment leave dents, chips, and gouges in stucco surfaces. We rebuild the damaged area to its original profile and match the surrounding texture and color.

Why Choose Us for Stucco Repair in Travis County?

  • We diagnose the root cause of every crack and stain, not just cover it up with a surface patch that fails again next season.
  • Our repair techniques use flexible bonding materials selected for the clay soil movement common across Travis County.
  • Every repair is custom color-matched and texture-matched so it blends seamlessly into your existing stucco finish.
  • We address underlying moisture and substrate problems before applying new material, preventing repeat failures.
  • All stucco repairs come with a written warranty so you have confidence the work will hold up.
  • We offer free on-site inspections throughout Travis County — call (512) 706-9699 to schedule yours.

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Travis County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our Stucco Repair Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Schedule Your Inspection

Call us or fill out our online form. We will set up a convenient time to visit your Travis County property and take a close look at the damage.

2

Get a Detailed Diagnosis

Our technician examines the stucco, tests for moisture, and identifies the root cause — whether it is soil movement, water intrusion, or aging material.

3

Review Your Repair Plan & Cost

We walk you through exactly what needs to be done, why, and what it will cost. No surprises and no pressure — just clear information so you can decide.

4

We Complete the Repair

Our crew removes the damaged stucco, fixes the substrate, and applies a color-matched finish that blends with the rest of your wall. We clean up and do a final walk-through with you.
